prompts: Model compression and quantization techniques include pruning, which removes unnecessary weights, and quantization, which reduces the precision of numbers the model uses, such as converting 32-bit floats to 8-bit integers. Knowledge distillation transfers knowledge from larger models to smaller ones, maintaining accuracy but using fewer resources. Clustering reduces model size by sharing weights among neurons. These methods optimize AI models for speed and power efficiency, crucial for deploying AI on edge devices with limited computational capacity. Balancing precision reduction and computation efficiency is vital to maintaining performance while enhancing resource efficiency.
